extraction.one_line_summary: HAAS Nathan timed his sprint perfectly on right-hand side after 14-rider selection over Al Jeel climb with 5km to go, beating VAN AVERMAET who led out too early
tactical_patterns: BMC burned matches controlling all day then VAN AVERMAET opened sprint too early, gifting positioning advantage to wheels
Al Jeel climb at 5km created decisive 14-rider split; late climbs eliminate pure sprinters even on rolling stages
HAAS specifically studied right-hand-side sprint positioning advantage rather than following BMC leadout train

rider_signals.HAAS Nathan: positive: demonstrated tactical homework studying road furniture while others relied on fitness, positioned away from favorites
rider_signals.VAN AVERMAET Greg: negative: BMC team control work appeared strong but Olympic champion mistimed sprint initiation after teammates exhausted
rider_signals.IRISARRI RINCON Marco: neutral: Trek veteran showed persistency in breakaway but solo continuation effort predictably failed
